content.text:
**`footer` is natively supported!** Neovim's `nvim_open_win` supports `footer` and `footer_pos` (similar to `title`/`title_pos`) — renders it right in the bottom border. That's much cleaner.

Let me remove the footer from `build_content` and use the native border footer instead.

local M = {}

local state = require("radar.state")
local tabs = require("radar.tabs")
local window = require("radar.window")

---Check if tabs window exists
---@return boolean
function M.exists()
  return state.is_tabs_window_valid()
end

---Close tabs window
---@return nil
function M.close()
  state.close_tabs_window()
end

---Build the content lines and line mapping
---@param tabs_data Radar.TabData[]
---@return string[], Radar.TabsLineMapping[]
local function build_content(tabs_data)
  local lines = {}
  local line_mapping = {}

  for _, tab in ipairs(tabs_data) do
    -- Tab header line
    local current_marker = tab.is_current and "*" or " "
    local tab_line = string.format("%sTab %d (%s)", current_marker, tab.index, tab.cwd)
    table.insert(lines, tab_line)
    table.insert(line_mapping, { tabid = tab.tabid })

    -- Buffer lines (indented)
    for _, buf in ipairs(tab.buffers) do
      local filepath = vim.fn.fnamemodify(buf.filepath, ":p:.")
      local buf_line = string.format("    %s", filepath)
      table.insert(lines, buf_line)
      table.insert(line_mapping, {
        tabid = tab.tabid,
        winid = buf.winid,
        filepath = buf.filepath,
      })
    end
  end

  -- Handle empty state
  if #lines == 0 then
    table.insert(lines, " No tabs with files")
    table.insert(line_mapping, {})
  end

  -- Footer with keyboard hints (no action mappings)
  table.insert(lines, "──────────────────────────────────────────────────")
  table.insert(line_mapping, {})
  table.insert(lines, "  [CR] jump  [x] close  [o] only  [q] quit")
  table.insert(line_mapping, {})

  return lines, line_mapping
end

---Apply highlights to the tabs buffer
---@param bufnr integer
---@param tabs_data Radar.TabData[]
local function apply_highlights(bufnr, tabs_data)
  local ns = vim.api.nvim_create_namespace("radar.tabs")
  vim.api.nvim_buf_clear_namespace(bufnr, ns, 0, -1)

  local lines = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_lines(bufnr, 0, -1, false)
  local line_idx = 0

  for _, tab in ipairs(tabs_data) do
    -- Highlight current tab header
    if tab.is_current and line_idx < #lines then
      vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(bufnr, ns, line_idx, 0, {
        end_col = #lines[line_idx + 1],
        hl_group = "@function",
      })
    end
    line_idx = line_idx + 1

    -- Buffer lines
    for _ in ipairs(tab.buffers) do
      line_idx = line_idx + 1
    end
  end

  -- Highlight footer lines (last 2 lines: separator + hint)
  local total_lines = #lines
  if total_lines >= 2 then
    for i = total_lines - 1, total_lines do
      local line = lines[i]
      if line and #line > 0 then
        vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(bufnr, ns, i - 1, 0, {
          end_col = #line,
          hl_group = "@comment",
        })
      end
    end
  end
end

---Setup keymaps for the tabs buffer
---@param bufnr integer
---@param config Radar.Config
local function setup_keymaps(bufnr, config)
  local opts = { buffer = bufnr, silent = true, noremap = true, nowait = true }

  -- Jump to tab/buffer
  vim.keymap.set("n", "<CR>", function()
    M.jump_to_line(config)
  end, vim.tbl_extend("force", opts, { desc = "Jump to tab/buffer" }))

  -- Close
  vim.keymap.set("n", "q", function()
    M.close()
  end, vim.tbl_extend("force", opts, { desc = "Close tabs window" }))

  vim.keymap.set("n", "<Esc>", function()
    M.close()
  end, vim.tbl_extend("force", opts, { desc = "Close tabs window" }))

  -- Delete buffer / close tab
  vim.keymap.set("n", "x", function()
    M.delete_line(config)
  end, vim.tbl_extend("force", opts, { desc = "Delete buffer/close tab" }))

  -- Make tab/buffer the only one
  vim.keymap.set("n", "o", function()
    M.only_line(config)
  end, vim.tbl_extend("force", opts, { desc = "Make tab/buffer the only one" }))
end

---Open the tabs floating window
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.open(config)
  -- Get tabs data
  local tabs_data = tabs.get_tabs_data()

  -- Build content
  local lines, line_mapping = build_content(tabs_data)
  state.tabs_line_mapping = line_mapping

  -- Create buffer
  local bufnr = vim.api.nvim_create_buf(false, true)
  vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("buftype", "nofile", { buf = bufnr })
  vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("bufhidden", "wipe", { buf = bufnr })
  vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("swapfile", false, { buf = bufnr })

  -- Set content
  vim.api.nvim_buf_set_lines(bufnr, 0, -1, false, lines)
  vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("modifiable", false, { buf = bufnr })

  -- Setup keymaps
  setup_keymaps(bufnr, config)

  -- Store buffer id
  state.tabs_bufid = bufnr

  -- Resolve window config from preset
  local win_config = window.resolve_config(config, config.tabs.win_preset, {
    title = "  TABS ",
  })

  local winid = vim.api.nvim_open_win(bufnr, true, win_config)

  -- Store window id
  state.tabs_winid = winid

  -- Apply window options
  for opt, value in pairs(config.tabs.win_opts) do
    vim.api.nvim_set_option_value(opt, value, { win = winid })
  end

  -- Apply highlights
  apply_highlights(bufnr, tabs_data)

  -- Position cursor on first line
  vim.api.nvim_win_set_cursor(winid, { 1, 0 })
end

---Toggle tabs window
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.toggle(config)
  if M.exists() then
    M.close()
  else
    M.open(config)
  end
end

---Update tabs window content
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.update(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  -- Get fresh data
  local tabs_data = tabs.get_tabs_data()
  local lines, line_mapping = build_content(tabs_data)
  state.tabs_line_mapping = line_mapping

  local bufnr = state.tabs_bufid
  if not bufnr or not vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(bufnr) then
    return
  end

  -- Update content
  vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("modifiable", true, { buf = bufnr })
  vim.api.nvim_buf_set_lines(bufnr, 0, -1, false, lines)
  vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("modifiable", false, { buf = bufnr })

  -- Re-apply highlights
  apply_highlights(bufnr, tabs_data)
end

---Delete the buffer or close the tab on the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.delete_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: close the window (not the buffer, which may be open elsewhere)
    local ok, err = pcall(vim.api.nvim_win_close, item.winid, false)
    if not ok then
      vim.notify("Cannot close window: " .. err, vim.log.levels.WARN)
      return
    end
  else
    -- Tab header line: close the tab
    local tab_count = #vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    if tab_count <= 1 then
      vim.notify("Cannot close the last tab", vim.log.levels.WARN)
      return
    end

    local ok, err = pcall(vim.cmd, "tabclose " .. vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_number(item.tabid))
    if not ok then
      vim.notify("Cannot close tab: " .. err, vim.log.levels.WARN)
      return
    end
  end

  -- Refresh the tabs window
  M.update(config)

  -- Clamp cursor if past the new last line
  if M.exists() and state.tabs_bufid and vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(state.tabs_bufid) then
    local new_count = vim.api.nvim_buf_line_count(state.tabs_bufid)
    if line_num > new_count then
      vim.api.nvim_win_set_cursor(state.tabs_winid, { math.max(1, new_count), 0 })
    end
  end
end

---Make the tab or buffer the only one (tabonly/only)
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.only_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Close the tabs window first
  M.close()

  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: switch to this window, then close all other windows in the tab
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
    vim.cmd("only")
  else
    -- Tab header line: switch to this tab, then close all other tabs
    local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
    vim.cmd("tabonly")
  end
end

---Jump to the tab/buffer on the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.jump_to_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Close window if auto_close is enabled
  if config.tabs.auto_close then
    M.close()
  end

  -- Jump to the target
  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: switch to specific window (this also switches tabs)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
  else
    -- Tab header line: switch to tab's active window
    local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
  end
end

return M

**Before (appended footer):**

**After (native border footer):**
The footer is now rendered in the **window's bottom border** via Neovim's native `footer`/`footer_pos` options on `nvim_open_win`:

```lua

})
```

It renders as part of the border itself, like:
```
┌── TABS ──────────────┐
│  *Tab 1 (src)        │
│      main.lua        │
│      utils.lua       │
│   Tab 2 (test)       │
│      main_spec.lua   │
└─ [CR] jump [x] close [o] only [q] quit ─┘
```

This is:
- **Always at the bottom** (it's part of the window border frame, like `title` is at the top)
- **Native** — no hacky content padding or separate windows
- **Clean** — no special line_mapping entries or highlight workarounds needed
- The `build_content` and `apply_highlights` functions are back to their original simplicity

function M.only_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Close the tabs window first
  M.close()

  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: switch to this window, then close all other windows in the tab

  M.update(config)

---Cut item state
local cut_item = nil

---Cut the tab or buffer on the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.cut_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: close the window, store filepath
    local filepath = item.filepath
    local ok, err = pcall(vim.api.nvim_win_close, item.winid, false)
    if not ok then
      vim.notify("Cannot close window: " .. err, vim.log.levels.WARN)
      return
    end
    cut_item = { type = "buffer", filepath = filepath, tabid = item.tabid }
  else
    -- Tab header line: close the tab, store tab info
    local tab_count = #vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    if tab_count <= 1 then
      vim.notify("Cannot close the last tab", vim.log.levels.WARN)
      return
    end

    -- Get all buffers in this tab before closing
    local buffers = {}
    local wins = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_list_wins(item.tabid)
    for _, winid in ipairs(wins) do
      local bufnr = vim.api.nvim_win_get_buf(winid)
      local filepath = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_name(bufnr)
      if filepath ~= "" then
        table.insert(buffers, { filepath = filepath, winid = winid })
      end
    end

    local ok, err = pcall(vim.cmd, "tabclose " .. vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_number(item.tabid))
    if not ok then
      vim.notify("Cannot close tab: " .. err, vim.log.levels.WARN)
      return
    end
    cut_item = { type = "tab", buffers = buffers }
  end

  -- Refresh the tabs window
  M.update(config)

  -- Clamp cursor
  if M.exists() and state.tabs_bufid and vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(state.tabs_bufid) then
    local new_count = vim.api.nvim_buf_line_count(state.tabs_bufid)
    if line_num > new_count then
      vim.api.nvim_win_set_cursor(state.tabs_winid, { math.max(1, new_count), 0 })
    end
  end
end

---Paste the cut item after the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.paste_line(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)

      vim.cmd("vsplit")

      vim.api.nvim_buf_set_option(cur_buf, 'buftype', '')

    else

      local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
      vim.cmd("tabedit " .. cut_item.filepath)

    -- Create a new tab with the stored buffers
    vim.cmd("tabnew")

      -- For now, just open the first buffer
      vim.cmd("edit " .. buf.filepath)
      break
    end
  end

  cut_item = nil
end

content.text: Let me think about this. You want `dd` to cut a line (buffer/tab) and `p` to paste it, so you can reorder tabs and move buffers between tabs visually.

local M = {}

local state = require("radar.state")
local tabs = require("radar.tabs")
local window = require("radar.window")

---Check if tabs window exists
---@return boolean
function M.exists()
  return state.is_tabs_window_valid()
end

---Close tabs window
---@return nil
function M.close()
  state.close_tabs_window()
end

---Build the content lines and line mapping
---@param tabs_data Radar.TabData[]
---@return string[], Radar.TabsLineMapping[]
local function build_content(tabs_data)
  local lines = {}
  local line_mapping = {}

  for _, tab in ipairs(tabs_data) do
    -- Tab header line
    local current_marker = tab.is_current and "*" or " "
    local tab_line =
      string.format("%sTab %d (%s)", current_marker, tab.index, tab.cwd)
    table.insert(lines, tab_line)
    table.insert(line_mapping, { tabid = tab.tabid })

    -- Buffer lines (indented)
    for _, buf in ipairs(tab.buffers) do
      local filepath = vim.fn.fnamemodify(buf.filepath, ":p:.")
      local buf_line = string.format("    %s", filepath)
      table.insert(lines, buf_line)
      table.insert(line_mapping, {
        tabid = tab.tabid,
        winid = buf.winid,
        filepath = buf.filepath,
      })
    end
  end

  -- Handle empty state
  if #lines == 0 then
    table.insert(lines, " No tabs with files")
    table.insert(line_mapping, {})
  end

  return lines, line_mapping
end

---Apply highlights to the tabs buffer
---@param bufnr integer
---@param tabs_data Radar.TabData[]
local function apply_highlights(bufnr, tabs_data)
  local ns = vim.api.nvim_create_namespace("radar.tabs")
  vim.api.nvim_buf_clear_namespace(bufnr, ns, 0, -1)

  local lines = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_lines(bufnr, 0, -1, false)
  local line_idx = 0

  for _, tab in ipairs(tabs_data) do
    -- Highlight current tab header
    if tab.is_current and line_idx < #lines then
      vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(bufnr, ns, line_idx, 0, {
        end_col = #lines[line_idx + 1],
        hl_group = "@function",
      })
    end
    line_idx = line_idx + 1

    -- Buffer lines
    for _ in ipairs(tab.buffers) do
      line_idx = line_idx + 1
    end
  end
end

---Setup keymaps for the tabs buffer
---@param bufnr integer
---@param config Radar.Config
local function setup_keymaps(bufnr, config)
  local opts = { buffer = bufnr, silent = true, noremap = true, nowait = true }

  -- Jump to tab/buffer
  vim.keymap.set("n", "<CR>", function()
    M.jump_to_line(config)
  end, vim.tbl_extend("force", opts, { desc = "Jump to tab/buffer" }))

  -- Close
  vim.keymap.set("n", "q", function()
    M.close()
  end, vim.tbl_extend("force", opts, { desc = "Close tabs window" }))

  vim.keymap.set("n", "<Esc>", function()
    M.close()
  end, vim.tbl_extend("force", opts, { desc = "Close tabs window" }))

  -- Delete buffer / close tab
  vim.keymap.set("n", "x", function()
    M.delete_line(config)
  end, vim.tbl_extend("force", opts, { desc = "Delete buffer/close tab" }))

  -- Make tab/buffer the only one
  vim.keymap.set("n", "o", function()
    M.only_line(config)
  end, vim.tbl_extend("force", opts, { desc = "Make tab/buffer the only one" }))

  -- New tab / vertical split
  vim.keymap.set("n", "n", function()
    M.new_line(config)
  end, vim.tbl_extend("force", opts, { desc = "New tab/vertical split" }))
end

---Open the tabs floating window
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.open(config)
  -- Get tabs data
  local tabs_data = tabs.get_tabs_data()

  -- Build content
  local lines, line_mapping = build_content(tabs_data)
  state.tabs_line_mapping = line_mapping

  -- Create buffer
  local bufnr = vim.api.nvim_create_buf(false, true)
  vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("buftype", "nofile", { buf = bufnr })
  vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("bufhidden", "wipe", { buf = bufnr })
  vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("swapfile", false, { buf = bufnr })

  -- Set content
  vim.api.nvim_buf_set_lines(bufnr, 0, -1, false, lines)
  vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("modifiable", false, { buf = bufnr })

  -- Setup keymaps
  setup_keymaps(bufnr, config)

  -- Store buffer id
  state.tabs_bufid = bufnr

  -- Resolve window config from preset
  local win_config = window.resolve_config(config, config.tabs.win_preset, {
    title = "  TABS ",
    footer = " [CR] jump  [x] close  [o] only  [n] new  [q] quit ",
    footer_pos = "left",
    border = "solid",
  })

  local winid = vim.api.nvim_open_win(bufnr, true, win_config)

  -- Store window id
  state.tabs_winid = winid

  -- Apply window options
  for opt, value in pairs(config.tabs.win_opts) do
    vim.api.nvim_set_option_value(opt, value, { win = winid })
  end

  -- Apply highlights
  apply_highlights(bufnr, tabs_data)

  -- Position cursor on first line
  vim.api.nvim_win_set_cursor(winid, { 1, 0 })
end

---Toggle tabs window
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.toggle(config)
  if M.exists() then
    M.close()
  else
    M.open(config)
  end
end

---Update tabs window content
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.update(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  -- Get fresh data
  local tabs_data = tabs.get_tabs_data()
  local lines, line_mapping = build_content(tabs_data)
  state.tabs_line_mapping = line_mapping

  local bufnr = state.tabs_bufid
  if not bufnr or not vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(bufnr) then
    return
  end

  -- Update content
  vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("modifiable", true, { buf = bufnr })
  vim.api.nvim_buf_set_lines(bufnr, 0, -1, false, lines)
  vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("modifiable", false, { buf = bufnr })

  -- Re-apply highlights
  apply_highlights(bufnr, tabs_data)
end

---Delete the buffer or close the tab on the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.delete_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: close the window (not the buffer, which may be open elsewhere)
    local ok, err = pcall(vim.api.nvim_win_close, item.winid, false)
    if not ok then
      vim.notify("Cannot close window: " .. err, vim.log.levels.WARN)
      return
    end
  else
    -- Tab header line: close the tab
    local tab_count = #vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    if tab_count <= 1 then
      vim.notify("Cannot close the last tab", vim.log.levels.WARN)
      return
    end

    local ok, err =
      pcall(vim.cmd, "tabclose " .. vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_number(item.tabid))
    if not ok then
      vim.notify("Cannot close tab: " .. err, vim.log.levels.WARN)
      return
    end
  end

  -- Refresh the tabs window
  M.update(config)

  -- Clamp cursor if past the new last line
  if
    M.exists()
    and state.tabs_bufid
    and vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(state.tabs_bufid)
  then
    local new_count = vim.api.nvim_buf_line_count(state.tabs_bufid)
    if line_num > new_count then
      vim.api.nvim_win_set_cursor(state.tabs_winid, { math.max(1, new_count), 0 })
    end
  end
end

---Make the tab or buffer the only one (tabonly/only)
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.only_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Close the tabs window first
  M.close()

  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: switch to this window, then close all other windows in the tab
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
    vim.cmd("only")
  else
    -- Tab header line: switch to this tab, then close all other tabs
    local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
    vim.cmd("tabonly")
  end
end

---Create a new tab or vertical split from the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.new_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Close the tabs window first
  M.close()

  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: create a vertical split in this window
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
    vim.cmd("vsplit")
  else
    -- Tab header line: create a new tab
    vim.cmd("tabnew")
  end
end

---Jump to the tab/buffer on the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.jump_to_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Close window if auto_close is enabled
  if config.tabs.auto_close then
    M.close()
  end

  -- Jump to the target
  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: switch to specific window (this also switches tabs)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
  else
    -- Tab header line: switch to tab's active window
    local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
  end
end

return M

---Cut item clipboard

---Cut the tab or buffer on the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.cut_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: close the window, store filepath
    local filepath = item.filepath
    if not filepath then
      return
    end
    local ok, err = pcall(vim.api.nvim_win_close, item.winid, false)
    if not ok then
      vim.notify("Cannot close window: " .. err, vim.log.levels.WARN)
      return
    end
    cut_item = { type = "buffer", filepath = filepath }
  else
    -- Tab header line: close the tab, store buffer filepaths
    local tab_count = #vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    if tab_count <= 1 then
      vim.notify("Cannot cut the last tab", vim.log.levels.WARN)
      return
    end

    -- Collect buffer filepaths before closing the tab
    local buffer_paths = {}
    local wins = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_list_wins(item.tabid)
    for _, winid in ipairs(wins) do
      local bufnr = vim.api.nvim_win_get_buf(winid)
      local filepath = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_name(bufnr)
      if filepath ~= "" then
        table.insert(buffer_paths, filepath)
      end
    end

    local ok, err = pcall(vim.cmd, "tabclose " .. vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_number(item.tabid))
    if not ok then
      vim.notify("Cannot cut tab: " .. err, vim.log.levels.WARN)
      return
    end
    cut_item = { type = "tab", buffers = buffer_paths }
  end

  -- Refresh the tabs window
  M.update(config)

  -- Clamp cursor if past the new last line
  if M.exists() and state.tabs_bufid and vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(state.tabs_bufid) then
    local new_count = vim.api.nvim_buf_line_count(state.tabs_bufid)
    if line_num > new_count then
      vim.api.nvim_win_set_cursor(state.tabs_winid, { math.max(1, new_count), 0 })
    end
  end
end

---Paste the cut item after the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.paste_line(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Close the tabs window before creating windows
  M.close()

  if cut_item.type == "buffer" then
    if item.winid then
      -- Buffer line: create a vertical split and open the buffer
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
      vim.cmd("vsplit")
      vim.cmd("edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    else
      -- Tab header line: open buffer in a new tab
      local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
      vim.cmd("tabedit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    end
  elseif cut_item.type == "tab" then
    if item.winid then
      -- Buffer line: add all tab's buffers as windows in this tab
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
      if #cut_item.buffers > 0 then
        vim.cmd("edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.buffers[1]))
        for i = 2, #cut_item.buffers do
          vim.cmd("vsplit")
          vim.cmd("edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.buffers[i]))
        end
      end
    else
      -- Tab header line: restore as a new tab
      if #cut_item.buffers > 0 then
        vim.cmd("tabedit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.buffers[1]))
        for i = 2, #cut_item.buffers do
          vim.cmd("vsplit")
          vim.cmd("edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.buffers[i]))
        end
      else
        vim.cmd("tabnew")
      end
    end
  end

  cut_item = nil
end

Same pre-existing failures only. Here's what was implemented:

**Cut (`dd`)** — `M.cut_line(config)`:
- **On a buffer line**: closes that window, stores the filepath in a module-level `cut_item` clipboard
- **On a tab header line**: collects all buffer filepaths in that tab, closes it, stores the list in `cut_item`
- Refreshes the tabs window and clamps cursor

**Paste (`p`)** — `M.paste_line(config)`:
- **Buffer in clipboard on a buffer line**: creates a vertical split, opens the file there
- **Buffer in clipboard on a tab line**: opens the file in a new tab
- **Tab in clipboard on a buffer line**: restores all the tab's buffers as windows in the current tab
- **Tab in clipboard on a tab line**: restores as a new tab
- Clears `cut_item` after paste

The tabs window stays open during cut operations (so you can move around and paste), and closes during paste (since windows/tabs are being created).

---Make the tab or buffer the only one (tabonly/only)
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.only_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Close the tabs window first
  M.close()

  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: switch to this window, then close all other windows in the tab
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
    vim.cmd("only")
  else
    -- Tab header line: switch to this tab, then close all other tabs
    local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
    vim.cmd("tabonly")
  end
end

---Create a new tab or vertical split from the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.new_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Close the tabs window first
  M.close()

  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: create a vertical split in this window
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
    vim.cmd("vsplit")
  else
    -- Tab header line: create a new tab
    vim.cmd("tabnew")
  end
end

---Cut the tab or buffer on the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.cut_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]

---Cut the tab or buffer on the current line (store in clipboard, don't close)
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.cut_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: store buffer info WITHOUT closing the window
    if not item.filepath then
      return
    end
    cut_item = { type = "buffer", filepath = item.filepath, winid = item.winid }
  else
    -- Tab header line: store tab info WITHOUT closing the tab
    local buffer_paths = {}
    local wins = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_list_wins(item.tabid)
    for _, winid in ipairs(wins) do
      local bufnr = vim.api.nvim_win_get_buf(winid)
      local filepath = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_name(bufnr)
      if filepath ~= "" then
        table.insert(buffer_paths, filepath)
      end
    end
    cut_item = { type = "tab", buffers = buffer_paths, tabid = item.tabid }
  end

  vim.notify("Cut " .. cut_item.type .. " — press p to paste it", vim.log.levels.INFO)
end

---Paste the cut item after the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.paste_line(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Close the original window/tab (from cut_item, not the current line)
  if cut_item.type == "buffer" and cut_item.winid then
    if vim.api.nvim_win_is_valid(cut_item.winid) then
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_win_close, cut_item.winid, false)
    end
  elseif cut_item.type == "tab" and cut_item.tabid then
    local tab_count = #vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    if
      tab_count > 1
      and vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(cut_item.tabid)
    then
      pcall(
        vim.cmd,
        "tabclose " .. vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_number(cut_item.tabid)
      )
    end
  end

  -- Create the new window/tab at the paste position
  if cut_item.type == "buffer" then
    if item.winid then
      -- Buffer line: create a vertical split and open the file
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "vsplit")
      pcall(vim.cmd, "edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    else
      -- Tab header line: open buffer in a new tab
      local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "tabedit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    end
  elseif cut_item.type == "tab" and #cut_item.buffers > 0 then
    if item.winid then
      -- Buffer line: add all buffers as windows in this tab
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
      vim.cmd("edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.buffers[1]))
      for i = 2, #cut_item.buffers do
        vim.cmd("vsplit")
        vim.cmd("edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.buffers[i]))
      end
    else
      -- Tab header line: restore as a new tab
      vim.cmd("tabedit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.buffers[1]))
      for i = 2, #cut_item.buffers do
        vim.cmd("vsplit")
        vim.cmd("edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.buffers[i]))
      end
    end
  end

  cut_item = nil

  -- Refresh the tabs window to show the new layout
  M.update(config)

  -- Clamp cursor if past the new last line
  if
    M.exists()
    and state.tabs_bufid
    and vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(state.tabs_bufid)
  then
    local new_count = vim.api.nvim_buf_line_count(state.tabs_bufid)
    if line_num > new_count then
      vim.api.nvim_win_set_cursor(state.tabs_winid, { math.max(1, new_count), 0 })
    end
  end
end

---Jump to the tab/buffer on the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.jump_to_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Close window if auto_close is enabled
  if config.tabs.auto_close then
    M.close()
  end

  -- Jump to the target
  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: switch to specific window (this also switches tabs)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
  else
    -- Tab header line: switch to tab's active window
    local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
  end
end

return M

---Paste the cut item before the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.paste_line_before(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  if not item.winid then
    -- Tab header line: navigate to the tab before, then paste after it
    local target_tabid = item.tabid
    local tabs = vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    local prev_tabid = nil
    for i, tabid in ipairs(tabs) do
      if tabid == target_tabid then
        if i > 1 then
          prev_tabid = tabs[i - 1]
        end
        break
      end
    end

    if prev_tabid then
      -- Switch to previous tab so paste after places it before target
      local prev_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(prev_tabid)
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(prev_win)
    end
  end

  -- Now paste after (which is effectively before the original position)
  M.paste_line(config)
end

function M.paste_line_before(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

        end
        break

edits.newText:
---Paste the cut item before the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.paste_line_before(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- For tab lines: remember the target position, then paste, then move before it
  local target_tabid = item.winid and nil or item.tabid

  -- Paste after first (creates the window/tab)
  M.paste_line(config)

  -- If it was a tab line and paste succeeded (cut_item cleared), move the new tab
  if target_tabid and not cut_item and vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(target_tabid) then
    local tabs = vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    local target_pos = nil
    for i, tabid in ipairs(tabs) do
      if tabid == target_tabid then
        target_pos = i - 1 -- 0-indexed position for tabmove
        break
      end
    end
    if target_pos and target_pos > 0 then
      -- The new tab is the current tab; move it before the target
      pcall(vim.cmd, "tabmove " .. (target_pos - 1))
    end
  end
end

However, there's a subtlety. The `P` keymap uses `vim.tbl_extend("force", opts, ...)` which includes `nowait = true`. But `nowait = true` means Neovim won't wait to see if the user presses another key after `P` - it immediately executes the mapping. For `P` this shouldn't be a problem since there's no longer mapping starting with `P`.

content.text:
Same pre-existing test failures only. Here's what changed:

**`dd`** — now just stores the cut data in memory without closing anything. The tabs window stays open and you get a notification. Move your cursor to the target position, then paste.

**`p`** — closes the **original** window/tab (from wherever you cut), creates a new one at the cursor position, then refreshes the tabs window to show the new layout. Window stays open throughout.

**`P`** — same as `p` but for tab lines it uses `tabmove` to place the new tab *before* the target tab.

The flow is now:
1. `dd` on a tab/buffer → item is marked (stored in clipboard, tabs window stays open)
2. Move cursor to where you want it
3. `p` → original is closed, new copy is created at cursor position, tabs window refreshes

content.text: You're right, the problem is that closing the original tab (especially if it's the current one) kills the floating window. Let me change the approach: instead of close+create, use `tabmove` to reposition tabs and `win_gotoid` + vsplit for buffers. No closing of anything that would invalidate the tabs window.

---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.paste_line(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Close the original window/tab (from cut_item, not the current line)
  if cut_item.type == "buffer" and cut_item.winid then
    if vim.api.nvim_win_is_valid(cut_item.winid) then
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_win_close, cut_item.winid, false)
    end
  elseif cut_item.type == "tab" and cut_item.tabid then
    local tab_count = #vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    if
      tab_count > 1
      and vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(cut_item.tabid)
    then
      pcall(
        vim.cmd,
        "tabclose " .. vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_number(cut_item.tabid)
      )
    end
  end

  -- Create the new window/tab at the paste position
  if cut_item.type == "buffer" then
    if item.winid then
      -- Buffer line: create a vertical split and open the file
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "vsplit")
      pcall(vim.cmd, "edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    else
      -- Tab header line: open buffer in a new tab
      local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "tabedit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    end
  elseif cut_item.type == "tab" and #cut_item.buffers > 0 then
    if item.winid then
      -- Buffer line: add all buffers as windows in this tab
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
      vim.cmd("edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.buffers[1]))
      for i = 2, #cut_item.buffers do
        vim.cmd("vsplit")
        vim.cmd("edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.buffers[i]))
      end
    else
      -- Tab header line: restore as a new tab
      vim.cmd("tabedit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.buffers[1]))
      for i = 2, #cut_item.buffers do
        vim.cmd("vsplit")
        vim.cmd("edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.buffers[i]))
      end
    end
  end

  cut_item = nil

  -- Refresh the tabs window to show the new layout
  M.update(config)

  -- Clamp cursor if past the new last line
  if
    M.exists()
    and state.tabs_bufid
    and vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(state.tabs_bufid)
  then
    local new_count = vim.api.nvim_buf_line_count(state.tabs_bufid)
    if line_num > new_count then
      vim.api.nvim_win_set_cursor(state.tabs_winid, { math.max(1, new_count), 0 })
    end
  end
end

---Paste the cut item before the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil

  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then

---Paste the cut item after the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.paste_line(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Save the current tabpage (where the floating window lives)
  local source_tabid = vim.api.nvim_get_current_tabpage()

  if cut_item.type == "buffer" then
    -- Close the original window
    if cut_item.winid and vim.api.nvim_win_is_valid(cut_item.winid) then
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_win_close, cut_item.winid, false)
    end

    -- Create a vertical split or new tab at the paste position
    if item.winid then
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "vsplit")
      pcall(vim.cmd, "edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    else
      local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "tabedit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    end

  elseif cut_item.type == "tab" and cut_item.tabid then
    if not vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(cut_item.tabid) then
      return
    end

    -- Calculate target position (0-indexed) for tabmove
    local tabs = vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    local target_pos = nil
    for i, tabid in ipairs(tabs) do
      if tabid == item.tabid then
        target_pos = i - 1 -- 0-indexed
        break
      end
    end

    if target_pos == nil then
      return
    end

    -- Navigate to the cut tab, move it after the target, then return
    local cut_tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(cut_item.tabid)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(cut_tab_win)
    vim.cmd("tabmove " .. (target_pos + 1))

    -- Return to the source tab where the floating window is
    if vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(source_tabid) then
      local source_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(source_tabid)
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_set_current_win, source_win)
    end
  end

  cut_item = nil

  -- Refresh the tabs window to show the new layout
  M.update(config)

  -- Clamp cursor if past the new last line
  if
    M.exists()
    and state.tabs_bufid
    and vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(state.tabs_bufid)
  then
    local new_count = vim.api.nvim_buf_line_count(state.tabs_bufid)
    if line_num > new_count then
      vim.api.nvim_win_set_cursor(state.tabs_winid, { math.max(1, new_count), 0 })
    end
  end
end

---Paste the cut item before the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.paste_line_before(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- For tab lines: remember the target position, then paste, then move before it
  local target_tabid = item.winid and nil or item.tabid

  -- Paste after first (creates the window/tab)
  M.paste_line(config)

  -- If it was a tab line and paste succeeded (cut_item cleared), move the new tab
  if target_tabid and not cut_item and vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(target_tabid) then
    local tabs = vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    local target_pos = nil
    for i, tabid in ipairs(tabs) do
      if tabid == target_tabid then
        target_pos = i - 1 -- 0-indexed position for tabmove
        break
      end
    end
    if target_pos and target_pos > 0 then
      -- The new tab is the current tab; move it before the target
      pcall(vim.cmd, "tabmove " .. (target_pos - 1))
    end
  end
end

---Paste the cut item before the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.paste_line_before(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Save the current tabpage (where the floating window lives)
  local source_tabid = vim.api.nvim_get_current_tabpage()

  if cut_item.type == "tab" and cut_item.tabid then
    if not vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(cut_item.tabid) then
      return
    end

    -- Calculate target position (0-indexed) for tabmove
    local tabs = vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    local target_pos = nil
    for i, tabid in ipairs(tabs) do
      if tabid == item.tabid then
        target_pos = i - 1 -- 0-indexed
        break
      end
    end

    if target_pos == nil then
      return
    end

    -- Navigate to the cut tab, move it before the target, then return
    local cut_tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(cut_item.tabid)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(cut_tab_win)
    vim.cmd("tabmove " .. target_pos)

    -- Return to the source tab where the floating window is
    if vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(source_tabid) then
      local source_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(source_tabid)
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_set_current_win, source_win)
    end

    cut_item = nil

    -- Refresh the tabs window to show the new layout
    M.update(config)

    -- Clamp cursor if past the new last line
    if
      M.exists()
      and state.tabs_bufid
      and vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(state.tabs_bufid)
    then
      local new_count = vim.api.nvim_buf_line_count(state.tabs_bufid)
      if line_num > new_count then
        vim.api.nvim_win_set_cursor(state.tabs_winid, { math.max(1, new_count), 0 })
      end
    end
  else
    -- Buffer line: fall back to paste after (for now)
    M.paste_line(config)
  end
end

Good, syntax checks pass. Let me do a quick review to ensure the logic is correct.

For `paste_line`:
1. Buffer paste: closes original window, creates vsplit/tabedit at cursor position
2. Tab paste: uses `tabmove` to reposition the tab after the target tab, no closing involved
3. Returns to source tabpage so the floating window stays visible
4. Refreshes the tabs window

For `paste_line_before`:
1. Tab paste: uses `tabmove` to reposition the tab before the target tab
2. Buffer paste: falls back to `paste_line` (paste after)
3. Refreshes the tabs window

The key improvement: tab paste now uses `tabmove` which doesn't close the tab at all. This means:
- The floating window stays open (we navigate to the source tab after the move)
- No data loss (the tab is moved, not deleted and recreated)
- The tabs window refreshes to show the updated order

For buffer paste, we still close the original window. But since we're closing a window within a tab (not the tab itself), the floating window on the current tabpage should remain intact. The only issue would be if the window being closed is on the current tabpage and is the same window... but since it was cut from a specific winid, it should be safe.

content.thinking:
The user is describing a significant architectural shift. Let me think about this carefully.

Current approach:
- The tabs window is a non-modifiable buffer that displays tab/buffer data
- Each line has a mapping (tabid, winid, filepath)
- Operations like dd, p, x, o use custom functions that manipulate Neovim's tab/window state

The user's proposed approach:
- The tabs window would be a modifiable buffer
- Each line has an ID
- User can edit/rearrange the buffer like a normal Neovim buffer
- A save event (like `:w` or `<C-s>`) parses the new order and applies it to Neovim's actual tab/window state

Let me think about the pros and cons:

**Pros of the proposed approach:**
- More flexible - users can use vim motions to rearrange (dd, p, visual selection, etc.)
- Less custom keymap logic
- More "vim-native" feel

**Cons:**
- Complex state management - mapping between display IDs and actual Neovim objects
- Hard to synchronize - after each save, Neovim's tab/buffer state changes, which affects the next render
- Error-prone - what if someone deletes a line? Or duplicates one? Or types garbage?
- The "parse the order of changed IDs" is actually quite complex to implement correctly
- What about the actual Neovim state changes during editing? If the user changes tabs while the window is open, the data becomes stale
- The `BufWriteCmd` or `TextChanged` events needed for this are non-trivial

**Pushback arguments:**
1. The current approach (custom keymaps) is actually the standard pattern for Neovim floating window UIs (Telescope, fzf-lua, etc. all use custom keymaps for special buffers)
2. A modifiable buffer with ID-based tracking introduces many edge cases:
   - What happens if a user types random text on a line?
   - What happens if a line is duplicated?
   - What happens if a tab/buffer is closed externally while editing?
   - The save/parse logic needs to handle all these gracefully
3. The current dd/p implementation with `tabmove` is actually quite clean - it's 3 key operations (dd to cut, move cursor, p to paste) vs. a complex save/parse cycle
4. Making the buffer modifiable means we can't use the simple `line_mapping` approach anymore - every line would need to carry an ID that survives edits

The current approach has a few bugs (like the buffer paste closing the window immediately), but those are fixable. The architectural change the user proposes is a much bigger undertaking with its own set of challenges.

2. Create a new vsplit
3. Open the file

content.text:
You're right on both counts — the buffer paste does have that issue, and the approach is getting unwieldy. Let me push back a bit on the modifiable-buffer-with-ID approach though, and then fix what we have.

**Why the modifiable-buffer approach is tricky:**

The tabs window reflects live Neovim state (actual windows, buffers, tabpages). Making it a free-form editable buffer means every change (typing, deleting, duplicating a line) would need to be validated and mapped back to real Neovim objects. It turns a few simple operations into a full parser with error handling for invalid states. That's why plugins like Telescope, fzf-lua, and nvim-tree all use the same pattern we have — a non-modifiable buffer with custom keymaps for structured operations.

**What I can fix right now though:**

The buffer paste closes too aggressively because it closes the original window *before* creating the new split. If the original window is on the current tabpage, that destabilizes things. The fix is to create the new split first, then clean up the original.

---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.paste_line(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Save the current tabpage (where the floating window lives)
  local source_tabid = vim.api.nvim_get_current_tabpage()

  if cut_item.type == "buffer" then
    -- Close the original window
    if cut_item.winid and vim.api.nvim_win_is_valid(cut_item.winid) then
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_win_close, cut_item.winid, false)
    end

    -- Create a vertical split or new tab at the paste position
    if item.winid then
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "vsplit")
      pcall(vim.cmd, "edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    else
      local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "tabedit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    end

  elseif cut_item.type == "tab" and cut_item.tabid then
    if not vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(cut_item.tabid) then
      return
    end

    -- Calculate target position (0-indexed) for tabmove
    local tabs = vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    local target_pos = nil
    for i, tabid in ipairs(tabs) do
      if tabid == item.tabid then
        target_pos = i - 1 -- 0-indexed
        break
      end
    end

    if target_pos == nil then
      return
    end

    -- Navigate to the cut tab, move it after the target, then return
    local cut_tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(cut_item.tabid)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(cut_tab_win)
    vim.cmd("tabmove " .. (target_pos + 1))

    -- Return to the source tab where the floating window is

I need to swap the order: create the new window first, then close the original.

But there's a subtlety: after we create the new vsplit and open the file, the new window becomes the current window. Then we try to close the original window (cut_item.winid). But the original window might have been affected by the vsplit (if it's on the same tabpage). For instance, if the original window is the one we vsplit from, then the vsplit already created a new window adjacent to it. Closing the original window after vsplit might close the wrong thing.

Actually, let me think about this more carefully. When we cut a buffer line, we get `cut_item = { type = "buffer", filepath = item.filepath, winid = item.winid }`. Where `item.winid` is the winid of the buffer that was cut.

When we paste on a buffer line:
- `item.winid` is the winid of the target window (where we want to paste)
- `cut_item.winid` is the winid of the original window (what was cut)

These could be different windows in different tabs, or even windows in the same tab.

If we create the new vsplit first at `item.winid`, that creates a new window. Then we close `cut_item.winid`. If `cut_item.winid` and `item.winid` are in the same tabpage, the vsplit might affect the layout, but closing `cut_item.winid` should still work.

But what if `cut_item.winid` == `item.winid`? That would mean cutting and pasting in the same position, which shouldn't normally happen.

Let me just swap the order:

```lua
if cut_item.type == "buffer" then
    -- Create a vertical split or new tab at the paste position FIRST
    if item.winid then
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "vsplit")
      pcall(vim.cmd, "edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    else
      local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "tabedit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    end

    -- THEN close the original window
    if cut_item.winid and vim.api.nvim_win_is_valid(cut_item.winid) then
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_win_close, cut_item.winid, false)
    end
end
```

This way, the new split is created first (establishing the paste target), and then the original window is closed. If they're in different tabpages, this is fine. If they're in the same tabpage, the new split is created and the original is closed — net effect is a move.

But wait, there's still an issue. When we `vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)`, this switches the current tabpage if `item.winid` is on a different tabpage. The floating tabs window is on the source tabpage. So after switching tabpages, the floating window becomes invisible.

Then we do `vsplit` and `edit` on this new tabpage. Then we try to close `cut_item.winid`, which might be on yet another tabpage. After `nvim_win_close`, we switch back... 

Actually, `nvim_win_close` doesn't switch tabpages. It closes the window on whatever tabpage it's on. If the window is on a different tabpage, the current tabpage doesn't change.

Let me restructure to be safer:

```lua
if cut_item.type == "buffer" then
    if item.winid then
      -- Navigate to target, create vsplit, open file
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "vsplit")
      pcall(vim.cmd, "edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    else
      -- Create new tab with the file
      local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "tabedit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    end

    -- Return to source tabpage (where the floating window is)
    if vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(source_tabid) then
      local source_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(source_tabid)
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_set_current_win, source_win)
    end

    -- Then close the original window
    if cut_item.winid and vim.api.nvim_win_is_valid(cut_item.winid) then
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_win_close, cut_item.winid, false)
    end
end
```

This way:
1. Create the new window first (on whatever tabpage)
2. Return to the source tabpage (where the floating window is)
3. Close the original window (which might be on a different tabpage, safe)

But wait, there's still an issue with step 3. If the original window is on the source tabpage (same as the floating window), closing it could affect the window layout. But since we already created the new split elsewhere, the original window is now redundant. If there are other windows on that tabpage, closing one is fine.

Actually, the real problem is: what if the original window IS the target window? That would happen if you cut buffer A and paste it on the same buffer line. But that doesn't make sense in practice.

content.text: OK

Syntax checks out. Here's the fix for the buffer paste:

**Before (broken):** Close original window → create new vsplit → open file → the floating window destabilizes if the original was on the same tabpage

**After (fixed):** Create new vsplit + open file first → return to source tabpage (where the floating window lives) → *then* close the original window. The original is now redundant since the buffer has been reopened elsewhere, so closing it is safe and the floating window is never disrupted.

Regarding your architectural question — I'd push back on the modifiable-buffer-with-ID approach. The issue is that the tabs window reflects **live Neovim state** (actual windows, buffers, tabpages). A free-form editable buffer means every character typed, every line deleted or duplicated needs to be validated against real Neovim objects. You'd need a save parser that handles invalid lines, duplicates, missing references, and stale state — it ends up being *more* complex than the custom keymap approach. Every plugin with a similar UI (Telescope, fzf-lua, nvim-tree) uses the same pattern: non-modifiable buffer + structured keymaps.

The specific bugs you're hitting (buffer paste closing things prematurely, `P` not mapped) are just implementation issues in the keymap approach, not fundamental flaws. Want me to commit this fix and see if the flow feels right now?

---@param bufnr integer
---@param tabs_data Radar.TabData[]
local function apply_highlights(bufnr, tabs_data)
  local ns = vim.api.nvim_create_namespace("radar.tabs")
  vim.api.nvim_buf_clear_namespace(bufnr, ns, 0, -1)

  local lines = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_lines(bufnr, 0, -1, false)
  local line_idx = 0

  for _, tab in ipairs(tabs_data) do
    -- Highlight current tab header
    if tab.is_current and line_idx < #lines then
      vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(bufnr, ns, line_idx, 0, {
        end_col = #lines[line_idx + 1],
        hl_group = "@function",
      })
    end
    line_idx = line_idx + 1

    -- Buffer lines
    for _ in ipairs(tab.buffers) do
      line_idx = line_idx + 1
    end
  end
end

---Setup keymaps for the tabs buffer
---@param bufnr integer
---@param config Radar.Config
local function setup_keymaps(bufnr, config)
  local opts = { buffer = bufnr, silent = true, noremap = true, nowait = true }

  -- Jump to tab/buffer
  vim.keymap.set("n", "<CR>", function()
    M.jump_to_line(config)
  end, vim.tbl_extend("force", opts, { desc = "Jump to tab/buffer" }))

  -- Close
  vim.keymap.set("n", "q", function()
    M.close()

function M.paste_line(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Save the current tabpage (where the floating window lives)
  local source_tabid = vim.api.nvim_get_current_tabpage()

  if cut_item.type == "buffer" then
    -- Create the new window FIRST at the paste position
    if item.winid then
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "vsplit")
      pcall(vim.cmd, "edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    else
      local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "tabedit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    end

    -- Return to source tabpage so the floating window stays visible
    if vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(source_tabid) then
      local source_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(source_tabid)
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_set_current_win, source_win)
    end

    -- THEN close the original window
    if cut_item.winid and vim.api.nvim_win_is_valid(cut_item.winid) then
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_win_close, cut_item.winid, false)
    end

  elseif cut_item.type == "tab" and cut_item.tabid then
    if not vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(cut_item.tabid) then
      return
    end

    -- Calculate target position (0-indexed) for tabmove
    local tabs = vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    local target_pos = nil
    for i, tabid in ipairs(tabs) do
      if tabid == item.tabid then
        target_pos = i - 1 -- 0-indexed
        break
      end
    end

    if target_pos == nil then
      return
    end

    -- Navigate to the cut tab, move it after the target, then return
    local cut_tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(cut_item.tabid)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(cut_tab_win)
    vim.cmd("tabmove " .. (target_pos + 1))

    -- Return to the source tab where the floating window is
    if vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(source_tabid) then
      local source_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(source_tabid)
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_set_current_win, source_win)
    end
  end

  cut_item = nil

  -- Refresh the tabs window to show the new layout
  M.update(config)

  -- Clamp cursor if past the new last line
  if
    M.exists()
    and state.tabs_bufid
    and vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(state.tabs_bufid)
  then
    local new_count = vim.api.nvim_buf_line_count(state.tabs_bufid)
    if line_num > new_count then
      vim.api.nvim_win_set_cursor(state.tabs_winid, { math.max(1, new_count), 0 })
    end
  end
end

function M.paste_line(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Save the current tabpage (where the floating window lives)
  local source_tabid = vim.api.nvim_get_current_tabpage()

  if cut_item.type == "buffer" then
    -- Create the new window FIRST at the paste position
    if item.winid then
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "vsplit")
      pcall(vim.cmd, "edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    else
      local tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(item.tabid)
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(tab_win)
      pcall(vim.cmd, "tabedit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    end

    -- Return to source tabpage so the floating window stays visible
    if vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(source_tabid) then
      local source_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(source_tabid)
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_set_current_win, source_win)
    end

    -- THEN close the original window
    if cut_item.winid and vim.api.nvim_win_is_valid(cut_item.winid) then
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_win_close, cut_item.winid, false)
    end

  elseif cut_item.type == "tab" and cut_item.tabid then
    if not vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(cut_item.tabid) then
      return
    end

    -- Calculate target position (0-indexed) for tabmove
    local tabs = vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    local target_pos = nil
    for i, tabid in ipairs(tabs) do
      if tabid == item.tabid then
        target_pos = i - 1 -- 0-indexed
        break
      end
    end

    if target_pos == nil then
      return
    end

    -- Navigate to the cut tab, move it after the target, then return
    local cut_tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(cut_item.tabid)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(cut_tab_win)
    vim.cmd("tabmove " .. (target_pos + 1))

    -- Return to the source tab where the floating window is
    if vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(source_tabid) then
      local source_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(source_tabid)
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_set_current_win, source_win)
    end
  end

  cut_item = nil
  cut_line_num = nil

  -- Refresh the tabs window to show the new layout
  M.update(config)

  -- Clamp cursor if past the new last line
  if
    M.exists()
    and state.tabs_bufid
    and vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(state.tabs_bufid)
  then
    local new_count = vim.api.nvim_buf_line_count(state.tabs_bufid)
    if line_num > new_count then
      vim.api.nvim_win_set_cursor(state.tabs_winid, { math.max(1, new_count), 0 })
    end
  end
end

---Paste the cut item before the current line
---@param config Radar.Config
---@return nil
function M.paste_line_before(config)
  if not M.exists() or not cut_item then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  -- Save the current tabpage (where the floating window lives)
  local source_tabid = vim.api.nvim_get_current_tabpage()

  if cut_item.type == "tab" and cut_item.tabid then
    if not vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(cut_item.tabid) then
      return
    end

    -- Calculate target position (0-indexed) for tabmove
    local tabs = vim.api.nvim_list_tabpages()
    local target_pos = nil
    for i, tabid in ipairs(tabs) do
      if tabid == item.tabid then
        target_pos = i - 1 -- 0-indexed
        break
      end
    end

    if target_pos == nil then
      return
    end

    -- Navigate to the cut tab, move it before the target, then return
    local cut_tab_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(cut_item.tabid)
    vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(cut_tab_win)
    vim.cmd("tabmove " .. target_pos)

    -- Return to the source tab where the floating window is
    if vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(source_tabid) then
      local source_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(source_tabid)
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_set_current_win, source_win)
    end

    cut_item = nil
    cut_line_num = nil

    -- Refresh the tabs window to show the new layout
    M.update(config)

    -- Clamp cursor if past the new last line
    if
      M.exists()
      and state.tabs_bufid
      and vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(state.tabs_bufid)
    then
      local new_count = vim.api.nvim_buf_line_count(state.tabs_bufid)
      if line_num > new_count then
      end
    end
  else
    -- Buffer line: fall back to paste after (for now)
    M.paste_line(config)
  end
end

function M.cut_line(config)
  if not M.exists() then
    return
  end

  local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(state.tabs_winid)
  local line_num = cursor[1]
  local item = state.tabs_line_mapping[line_num]

  if not item or not item.tabid then
    return
  end

  if item.winid then
    -- Buffer line: store buffer info WITHOUT closing the window
    if not item.filepath then
      return
    end
    cut_item = { type = "buffer", filepath = item.filepath, winid = item.winid }
  else
    -- Tab header line: store tab info WITHOUT closing the tab
    local buffer_paths = {}
    local wins = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_list_wins(item.tabid)
    for _, winid in ipairs(wins) do
      local bufnr = vim.api.nvim_win_get_buf(winid)
      local filepath = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_name(bufnr)
      if filepath ~= "" then
        table.insert(buffer_paths, filepath)
      end
    end
    cut_item = { type = "tab", buffers = buffer_paths, tabid = item.tabid }
  end

content.text:
Syntax and tests pass. Here's how it works now:

- **`dd` on a line** → stores the cut data and sets `cut_line_num` to that line number. `apply_highlights` is re-run, which dims that line with the `@comment` highlight group
- **`p` / `P`** → performs the move, then clears both `cut_item` and `cut_line_num`. The next `M.update()` refresh re-applies highlights without the dimmed line
- If you close and reopen the tabs window, the cut mark is naturally gone (state is cleared)

The dimming uses `@comment` which is a built-in highlight group — it'll adapt to whatever color scheme you're using.

2. `paste_line`: 
   a. Creates vsplit at target position
   b. Opens the file in the new split
   c. Returns to source tabpage
   d. Tries to close `cut_item.winid`
   e. Refreshes tabs window

If step d fails (e.g., the window was already closed, or the winid is invalid), the buffer would appear in both places. That could be the regression.

Or maybe the issue is that when we say `pcall(vim.cmd, "edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))`, it opens the file but the buffer was already open in the original window. So Neovim just focuses the existing buffer. Then when we close the original window, the buffer is still open in the new split.

Actually, `:edit filename` in Neovim opens the file in the current window. If the file is already open in another window, it navigates to that existing buffer. It doesn't create a new buffer. So `pcall(vim.cmd, "edit " .. cut_item.filepath)` would open the existing buffer in the new vsplit.

That should be fine. The new split shows the buffer, and then we close the original window. Net effect: the buffer moved from the original window to the new split.

But wait, there's another subtlety. After `pcall(vim.cmd, "vsplit")`, we're in the new split. Then `pcall(vim.cmd, "edit " .. filepath)` opens the file in that new split. But if the file is already open in another window on the same tabpage (e.g., the original window), `:edit` might switch to that existing window instead of opening it in the current window. Actually no, `:edit` always opens in the current window. It's `:buffer` or just clicking the buffer that switches. `:edit filename` opens the file in the current window, regardless of where else it's shown.

I think the code is correct but there might be an edge case. Let me just ask the user what specifically is wrong.

Actually, let me re-read the user's message one more time: "hmm yeah but now you have a regression. p does open the buffer immediately and leaves the window open"

I think they're saying:
1. The cut line visual highlight works (they said "yeah" to my description)
2. BUT there's a regression: pressing `p` opens the buffer immediately and leaves the tabs window open
3. They might want the tabs window to close after paste

But they literally said "Don't close the window on p please" earlier. So I'm confused.

Actually wait - maybe the "regression" is that the paste happens immediately without giving the user a chance to confirm. They said earlier "Also for these modifications the windows should not close immedatily, since I want to confirm my selection." Maybe they want some kind of confirmation before the paste executes?

Or maybe they want the tabs window to stay open during the cut/paste workflow (which it does now), but they want it to close after the paste is complete?

Or maybe the issue is different: when `p` is pressed, it creates the vsplit immediately and the user sees the file content flash behind the tabs window. This might be disorienting.

I think the best approach is to just ask the user what they mean by "regression" and what they expected to happen differently.

Let me understand the issue:

1. User cuts a buffer line (dd) - stores the buffer info
2. User moves cursor to a buffer in the first tab
3. User presses p (paste)
4. The paste works - but it opens and focuses the pasted buffer immediately
5. The tabs window stays open (which is good)

The problem is step 4 - the paste action is switching focus to the pasted buffer. The user probably wants to stay in the tabs window to continue working (maybe paste more things or do other operations).

Looking at the code in `paste_line` for buffer paste:

```lua
if cut_item.type == "buffer" then
    -- Create the new window FIRST at the paste position
    if item.winid then
      vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)  -- <-- This switches focus to the target window
      pcall(vim.cmd, "vsplit")
      pcall(vim.cmd, "edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(cut_item.filepath))
    else
      ...
    end

    -- Return to source tabpage so the floating window stays visible
    if vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(source_tabid) then
      local source_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(source_tabid)
      pcall(vim.api.nvim_set_current_win, source_win)  -- <-- This should return to tabs window
    end

    -- THEN close the original window
    ...
```

Wait, it does return to the source tabpage after creating the new split. So the focus should go back to the source tabpage where the tabs window lives.

But the "source tabpage" might not be the tabs window itself. The `source_tabid` is saved at the beginning of `paste_line` as `vim.api.nvim_get_current_tabpage()`. This is the tabpage where the tabs floating window is displayed. But when we `vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)`, we switch to the target tabpage (where we want to paste). Then after creating the split, we go back to source_tabid.

But wait - `source_tabid` is the tabpage where the tabs window was created. The tabs floating window is on that tabpage. But `source_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(source_tabid)` gets some window on that tabpage, not necessarily the tabs window specifically.

Actually, more importantly, after we `pcall(vim.api.nvim_set_current_win, source_win)`, we switch focus back to the source tabpage. But the tabs window is a floating window - it should still be visible. And since the tabs window is open, the cursor should be in the tabs window buffer... unless `nvim_set_current_win` on the source tabpage focuses a non-floating window on that tabpage, not the floating window itself.

Hmm, I think the issue is that `source_win` is just some window on the source tabpage (not the tabs floating window). When we focus that window, the tabs floating window might lose focus or the paste buffer might have already been focused by the `edit` command.

Actually, let me re-read the flow:

1. `source_tabid = vim.api.nvim_get_current_tabpage()` - save the tabpage where the tabs window is
2. `vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)` - switch to the target window (on potentially another tabpage)

5. Return to source tabpage: `source_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(source_tabid)` - get a window on source tabpage
6. `vim.api.nvim_set_current_win(source_win)` - focus that window

Step 6 focuses a regular window on the source tabpage, but NOT the tabs floating window. The tabs floating window would become the focus... actually, I'm not sure.

Wait, the tabs window has `focusable = true` (from the window preset). But when we call `nvim_set_current_win(source_win)`, it sets the current window to `source_win` which is a regular editor window. The tabs floating window isn't focused.

After returning to the source tabpage, the user would need to press some key to navigate back to the tabs window. But since the tabs window is still open and visible, the user can interact with it.

Actually, hmm, I think the real issue might be different. Let me re-read what the user said: "it also opens and focuses the pasted buffer immediately". So the pasted buffer gets focus. That's because of step 4 (`edit file`) - it opens the file and the new split becomes the current window.

But then we return to the source tabpage (step 6), so focus should go back to the source tabpage. But maybe the user is seeing the focus switch briefly and then come back? Or maybe the return to source tabpage isn't working properly?

Actually, I think the issue might be that when we do `nvim_set_current_win(item.winid)` in step 2, this switches to the target tabpage. The tabs floating window is on the source tabpage, so it becomes invisible. Then we do vsplit and edit. Then we go back to source tabpage.

But wait - when we go back to the source tabpage (steps 5-6), the tabs floating window should become visible again. And `nvim_set_current_win(source_win)` focuses a regular window on that tabpage, but the tabs floating window should also be visible with focusable=true.

Actually, I wonder if the issue is simpler. Maybe the user expects that after paste, the focus stays in the tabs window (the floating window). But currently, after returning to the source tabpage, the focus is on `source_win` (a regular editor window), not the floating window.

Hmm, but I'm not 100% sure what's happening. Let me think about what "leaves the window open (which is correct) but it also opens and focuses the pasted buffer immediately" means.

I think the user is saying:
1. After pressing `p`, they see the pasted buffer open (in the background or foreground)
2. The tabs window stays open (good)
3. But the focus switches to the pasted buffer, away from the tabs window

So the issue is that after paste, the focus is on the newly created split showing the pasted buffer, not on the tabs floating window.

The fix would be to restore focus to the tabs floating window after the paste operation, rather than just going back to the source tabpage.

```lua
-- Return to source tabpage so the floating window stays visible
if vim.api.nvim_tabpage_is_valid(source_tabid) then
  local source_win = vim.api.nvim_tabpage_get_win(source_tabid)
  pcall(vim.api.nvim_set_current_win, source_win)
end
```

This goes back to `source_win` which is the first window on the source tabpage (not the floating window). To focus the floating window, I should use `state.tabs_winid` instead.

Also, for tab paste (`tabmove`), we also navigate away from the floating window and back. But tabmove navigates to the cut tab, moves it, then returns to the source tabpage. The same issue would apply.

Let me fix both cases to restore focus to the tabs floating window.
